title: University of Strasbourg moves to expel international students over unpaid tuition
subhead: The university is preparing to drop around 40 master's students who have been unable to pay the nearly €4,000 increased tuition fees required of non-EU students. Few universities have enforced this measure. The government wants that to change.

title: Iran's uncomfortable position, between fragile ceasefire and blockade standoff
Second French peacekeeper dies after ambush blamed on Hezbollah
French right-wing leader's call to ostracize Spain over migration policy sparks backlash
subhead: On Monday, Bruno Retailleau, the Les Républicains-backed candidate for France's 2027 presidential election, said he wanted to isolate Spain and its Socialist leader over a sweeping plan to regularize undocumented migrants.

title: In 'Michael,' Michael Jackson is depicted only as musical phenomenon, far from accusations
subhead: Antoine Fuqua's biopic sidesteps any controversy by focusing on the singer's early career, but stands out for the way it recreates the electricity of his live performances.

title: 'Bookshops now serve as sentinels of resistance to the radical rightward shift of society'
subhead: Christian Thorel, a prominent figure in independent book selling, was among the first to sound the alarm about billionaire Vincent Bolloré's ideological grip on the publishing sector, starting with the acquisition of France's top publishing group Hachette, in 2023.

title: A neglected jewel, France's dilapidated National Museum of Natural History calls for help
subhead: The institution boasts strong visitor numbers and an international scientific reputation. But its head warns of catastrophic conditions, including mold in the collections, a flooded library, and cracks in the walls.

Neither war nor peace: Trump unilaterally extends ceasefire with Iran
subhead: The US president has not set a new deadline for Iran, instead waiting for the various political and security factions within the Iranian regime to reach a 'unified proposal.'

title: France's business elite warms to the far right
subhead: Leaders of France's largest companies are no longer shying away from meetings with the Rassemblement National's leaders, as they seek to influence the economic agenda of one of the leading contenders for the 2027 presidential election.

title: France's print media in crisis amid massive layoffs
subhead: Since December 2025, magazine and regional press companies have announced nearly 1,000 job cuts, putting the viability of countless newsrooms at risk.
